title: FDA Variance Applications
description: Procedures for submitting and maintaining FDA variance applications for laser light shows and laser products
learning_objectives: Understand the variance application requirements for laser light shows
Identify required forms and documentation for variance submissions
Know the annual reporting requirements and deadlines

content_sections.title: Variance Application Overview
content_sections.content: To apply for a variance to request approval of a different way to provide the same safety that the FDA mandatory performance standard requirements require, you need to submit a variance request to CDRH (Center for Devices and Radiological Health) and await an approval letter.
content_sections.plain_language_summary: Think of a variance like getting permission to take a different route to the same destination. You're telling FDA 'I can't follow the standard rule exactly, but my alternative method is just as safe.'
content_sections.analogy: It's similar to getting a building variance - you need approval BEFORE construction begins, not after.
key_points: Variance allows alternative compliance methods that provide equivalent safety
Applies to Class IIIB/3B, Class IV/4, or Risk Group 3 laser products

content_sections.title: Product Types Requiring Variance
content_sections.content: The following laser product types require variance applications under 21 CFR 1040.10(b)(13):

product_types.description: Entertainment laser displays using laser projectors
Devices that project laser light for display purposes
Stage lighting instruments using laser illumination
Other laser products used for demonstration purposes

content_sections.title: Required Forms by Product Type
content_sections.content: Different entity types require different form submissions:

form_definitions.form_3147: Variance Application Form - Main application for variance request
form_definitions.form_3640: Laser Light Show Report - Detailed show safety information
form_definitions.form_3632: Laser Product Report - Product specifications and testing data

form_selection_decision_tree.question: Which forms do YOU need? Follow this guide:

branches.tip: Even using existing lasers makes you a manufacturer!

branches.if: You manufacture laser projectors for sale
branches.tip: All three forms required for product manufacturers
No product report (3632) needed for dealers
Simplest application - just the main form
Temporary needs show report for each setup

content_sections.title: Annual Reporting Requirements
content_sections.content: Current variance holders must submit annual reports to maintain their variance status.

key_requirements.details: Submit Form FDA 3636 by September 1st each year
Annual report submission automatically extends variance approval for another year
If variance expired due to missed annual report, submit cover sheet for renewal request

content_sections.title: Variance Application Checklist
content_sections.content: Complete all items before submitting your variance application package:

checklist_items.item: All applicable sections of all required forms/documents are completed
checklist_items.why_it_matters: Incomplete forms cause delays or rejection
checklist_items.tip: Have a colleague double-check your work

checklist_items.item: The variance applicant's (manufacturer's) email address is provided on all forms
checklist_items.why_it_matters: FDA may need to contact you for clarifications
checklist_items.tip: Use a business email you check regularly

checklist_items.item: All forms are signed by the manufacturer
All forms include at least one make and model of laser product in the applicable section
checklist_items.tip: List all models you plan to use

checklist_items.item: If submitter is different than manufacturer, submitter contact information and email are included
checklist_items.why_it_matters: FDA needs to know who's handling the application
checklist_items.tip: Include both manufacturer and consultant info if applicable

checklist_items.item: If requesting renewal or amendment, include the Variance Number (yyyy-V-xxxx)
checklist_items.why_it_matters: FDA can't match amendment to existing variance without this
checklist_items.tip: Always copy the exact format from your approval letter

common_mistakes.mistake: Waiting until the last minute before a show
common_mistakes.consequence: Variance won't be approved in time - shows may be cancelled
common_mistakes.solution: Apply at least 3 months before first planned use
Set calendar reminders for August 1st and August 15th

common_mistakes.mistake: Submitting wrong forms for your role
common_mistakes.consequence: Application returned for correction - delays approval
common_mistakes.solution: Use the Form Selection Decision Tree above

common_mistakes.mistake: Not including variance number on amendments
common_mistakes.consequence: FDA can't match amendment to existing variance

content_sections.title: Submission Procedure
content_sections.content: Follow these steps to submit your variance application:

steps.details: Complete all required forms and include the cover sheet checklist
Send as separate PDF attachments to RadHealthCustomerService@fda.hhs.gov
Include applicant's firm name in email subject line
Include applicant's firm name and date in filename

processing_info.acknowledgement: CDRH/DCC issues acknowledgement letters with Accession numbers
processing_info.docket: FDA Dockets Management Staff (DMS) issues Docket number
processing_info.note: Do not separately send to DMS - CDRH forwards applications automatically

content_sections.title: Types of Variance Requests
content_sections.content: Three types of variance requests can be submitted:

types.description: New variance application for first-time approval
types.requirements: Complete application with all required forms
Include Variance Number; update Form 3640 only if previously submitted and needs updating; include cover letter and any missing annual reports
Include Variance Number; update Form 3632 and/or Form 3640 only if previously submitted and need updating; include cover letter with requested amendments

content_sections.title: Manufacturer Definition for Laser Light Shows
content_sections.content: The act of assembly of a laser light show using a previously manufactured laser or laser product results in the creation or manufacture of a 'new' product. The creation of this new product may involve the addition of such components as display screens, mirrors, smoke/fog and optics but may result from merely changing the intent and use of the original laser. Hence, the person who produces the 'new' product is considered a 'manufacturer' if such person is engaged in the business of manufacturing laser light shows.

content_sections.title: Real-World Application Examples
content_sections.content: See how variance requirements apply in actual scenarios:

examples.situation: Laser Productions Inc. wants to use custom-built 15W RGB laser projectors for a nationwide concert tour
examples.action: Must submit Form 3147 + Form 3640 as a Light Show Producer
examples.outcome: Receives variance 2024-V-1234, valid for all shows under their annual report
examples.key_lesson: Tour companies are considered manufacturers and need full variance documentation

examples.situation: Club Galaxy wants to install laser-illuminated lighting permanently
examples.action: Only needs Form 3147 as permanent LIP user
examples.outcome: Lower compliance burden because installation doesn't move
examples.key_lesson: Permanent installations have simpler requirements than temporary or mobile setups

examples.situation: TechLasers Inc. developing new 25W laser projector for sale
examples.action: Must submit all three forms: 3147, 3640, AND 3632
examples.outcome: Product can be sold to dealers once variance approved
examples.key_lesson: Product manufacturers have the most extensive documentation requirements

content_sections.title: Application Process Timeline
content_sections.content: From submission to approval - what to expect:

timeline.your_action: Ensure all required forms are complete and accurate
Include firm name in subject line; use proper filenames
Wait for acknowledgment letter with Accession number
Respond promptly to any FDA requests for clarification
File approval letter; mark September 1st on calendar for annual reports

variance.definition: Approval for alternative compliance method providing equivalent safety
variance.plain_english: Official permission to use a different safety method than the standard rule
variance.analogy: Like getting a building variance - different approach, same safety outcome

CDRH.definition: Center for Devices and Radiological Health - FDA division handling laser products
CDRH.plain_english: The FDA office that acts as the 'traffic cop' for all laser products in the US

LLS.definition: Laser Light Show - Entertainment laser display

LIP.plain_english: Projector that uses lasers instead of traditional lamps for illumination

LILI.plain_english: Stage lighting that uses laser technology

Accession Number.plain_english: Your application's tracking number - like a FedEx tracking number for your paperwork
Accession Number.use: Reference this number in all communications about your application

Docket Number.definition: Tracking number assigned by FDA Dockets Management Staff
Docket Number.plain_english: The public record number - allows anyone to look up your variance request
Docket Number.use: Used for public transparency and future amendments

Laser Notice.definition: FDA's official guidance documents clarifying regulations
Laser Notice.plain_english: Like 'clarification memos' from FDA explaining how to interpret rules

relevant_notices.Laser Notice #22: Defines who is a 'manufacturer' for light shows
